messages.content: You are a senior HVAC controls engineer extracting official ASHRAE Guideline 36 knowledge from a focused chapter/section. Use the provided standard context pack for definitions, cross-references, and shared control philosophy. The context pack is CONTEXT ONLY: do not extract candidates from it and do not use it as the evidence source. Extract candidates only from FOCUS SECTION TEXT, and every evidence_quote must be copied from FOCUS SECTION TEXT. Treat chunks only as evidence anchors, not extraction boundaries. FOCUS SCOPE: Extract ONLY knowledge whose primary section_id equals one of these sections or starts with one of these section prefixes followed by a dot: 5.19. Use the rest of the manual only as context for definitions, cross-references, and terminology. Do not return candidates from outside the focus scope. Evidence quotes must come from the focus scope text, not from unrelated sections used only as context. Allowed knowledge_type values: operational_sequence, parameter_spec, fault_diagnostic_rule, commissioning_step, application_guidance. Prefer high-value operational knowledge: control sequences, enable/disable logic, reset logic, staging logic, setpoints, timers, alarm/fault diagnostic rules, commissioning/TAB checks, and application rules. Do not extract front matter, legal text, table-of-contents lines, bibliography, generic definitions without operational use, or unsupported paraphrases. If the same knowledge appears multiple times, return it once with the clearest authoritative evidence_quote. evidence_quote MUST be a verbatim contiguous substring copied from the manual and it MUST support every important fact in summary, trigger_condition, required_behavior, configurable_values, fault_condition, or commissioning_check. For operational_sequence, quote the complete rule sentence or table row that includes the condition and commanded behavior. For fault_diagnostic_rule, quote the alarm or fault condition itself. For commissioning_step, quote the check or verification action. A section heading alone, a half sentence, an editor instruction such as retain/delete this section, or an introductory phrase is NOT sufficient evidence. Do not use ellipses. Do not combine non-contiguous bullets. If one exact quote cannot support the candidate, make the candidate narrower or skip it. If a requirement has alternatives, split them into separate candidates unless one exact quote supports the combined item. Return at most 40 candidates. Prefer fewer high-confidence candidates with complete evidence over broad coverage with weak evidence. Keep evidence_quote concise, usually one complete sentence or one complete table row, not a paragraph. Preserve section_id and page_hint from nearby headings and [[page=...]] anchors. Return strict JSON with fields candidates and skipped_reason only.
[redacted: ASHRAE section text omitted from artifact]

message.reasoning_content:
We are asked to extract knowledge from section 5.19 only (focus_sections: 5.19). The provided context pack includes section 5.1 and 3, but those are context only, not for extraction. We must extract candidates from the focus section text under [[requested_section=5.19 page_range=171-171 title=5.19 General Constant Speed Exhaust Fan]]. The text there is:

5.19.1. Exhaust Fan Control
5.19.1.1. Exhaust Fan Start/Stop
The engineer must select between control options:
If the exhaust fan is to be operated with associated Zone Groups, keep subsection “5.19.1.1.a” and delete subsection “b”
If the exhaust fan is to be cycled to maintain room temperature, keep subsection “b” and delete subsection “a”.
a. Exhaust fan shall operate when any of the associated system supply fans is proven on and any associated Zone Group  is in the Occupied Mode. See Section 3.1.3 for Zone Group assignments.
b. Exhaust fan shall run when zone temperature rises above the active cooling setpoint until zone temperature falls more than 1°C (2°F) below the active cooling setpoint for 2 minutes.
The room temperature control method should only be used in non-occupied spaces where ventilation is not required (e.g., equipment rooms).

5.19.2. Alarms
5.19.2.1. Maintenance interval alarm when fan has operated for more than 3,000 hours: Level 4. Reset interval counter when alarm is acknowledged.
5.19.2.2. Fan alarm is indicated by the status being different from the command for a period of 15 seconds.
a. Commanded on, status off: Level 2
b. Commanded off, status off: Level 4

We need to extract knowledge of allowed types: operational_sequence, parameter_spec, fault_diagnostic_rule, commissioning_step, application_guidance. We should not extract front matter, legal, etc. The text includes operational sequences for exhaust fan start/stop, alarms, and application guidance about room temperature control method. Let's parse:

- 5.19.1.1 has two exclusive control options (a and b) for exhaust fan start/stop. That is operational_sequence.
- The note about "room temperature control method should only be used in non-occupied spaces ..." is application_guidance.
- 5.19.2.1 is a maintenance alarm: fault_diagnostic_rule.
- 5.19.2.2 is a fan alarm rule: fault_diagnostic_rule.
- The introductory line "The engineer must select between control options:" is not a candidate, it's an instruction.

Thus, we can extract:
1. Operational sequence: Exhaust fan operates when supply fan proven on and Zone Group in Occupied mode (option a). 
2. Operational sequence: Exhaust fan cycles based on zone temperature (option b).
3. Application guidance: Room temperature control method only for non-occupied spaces.
4. Fault diagnostic: Maintenance interval alarm at 3000 hours, Level 4, resets on acknowledgment.
5. Fault diagnostic: Fan status mismatch alarm after 15 seconds, with levels for commanded on/status off (Level 2) and commanded off/status off (Level 4).

We must provide one candidate per distinct knowledge item. Each must have evidence_quote from the focus section text, and the quote must be verbatim contiguous. We'll structure candidates accordingly.

Let's number:

Candidate 1: Option a start/stop rule. knowledge_type: operational_sequence, title: "Exhaust Fan Start/Stop Based on Zone Group Occupancy", section_id: "5.19.1.1", section_title: "Exhaust Fan Start/Stop", equipment_scope: "exhaust fan", summary: "Exhaust fan shall operate when any associated system supply fan is proven on and any associated Zone Group is in Occupied Mode.", trigger_condition: "supply fan proven on and Zone Group in Occupied Mode", required_behavior: "exhaust fan runs", configurable_values: [], evidence_quote: "a. Exhaust fan shall operate when any of the associated system supply fans is proven on and any associated Zone Group  is in the Occupied Mode .  See Secti on 3.1.3  for Zone Group assignments." (the quote has spacing issues but we capture exactly). page_hint: 171 (based on range 171-171). confidence: 1.

Candidate 2: Option b start/stop rule: operational_sequence, title: "Exhaust Fan Start/Stop Based on Room Temperature", summary: "Exhaust fan shall run when zone temperature rises above the active cooling setpoint until zone temperature falls more than 1°C (2°F) below the active cooling setpoint for 2 minutes.", trigger_condition: "zone temperature > active cooling setpoint", required_behavior: "exhaust fan runs; stops when zone temperature falls more than 1°C (2°F) below active cooling setpoint for 2 minutes", evidence_quote: "b. Exhaust fan shall run when zone temperature rises above the active cooling setpoint until zone temperature falls more than 1°C (2°F) below the active cooling setpoint for 2 minutes." page_hint: 171, confidence: 1.

Candidate 3: Application guidance: "The room temperature control method should only be used in non-occupied spaces where ventilation is not required (e.g., equipment rooms)." knowledge_type: application_guidance, title: "Room Temperature Control Method Application", section_id: "5.19.1.1", summary: "Room temperature control method should only be used in non-occupied spaces where ventilation is not required (e.g., equipment rooms).", evidence_quote: "The room temperature control method should only be used in non -occupied spaces where ventilation is not required (e.g., equipment rooms)." page_hint: 171.

Candidate 4: Fault diagnostic: maintenance interval alarm for fan runtime. knowledge_type: fault_diagnostic_rule, title: "Fan Maintenance Interval Alarm", section_id: "5.19.2.1", summary: "Alarm when fan has operated for more than 3,000 hours: Level 4. Reset interval counter when alarm is acknowledged.", fault_condition: "fan run hours > 3000", alarm_level: Level 4, required_behavior: "reset interval counter on acknowledgment", evidence_quote: "Maintenance interval alarm when fan has operated for mo re than 3,000 hours:  Level 4.  Reset interval counter when alarm is acknowledged." (the text has "mo re" likely a scan artifact, but we quote exactly). page_hint: 171.

Candidate 5: Fault diagnostic: fan status mismatch alarm. This is from 5.19.2.2: "Fan alarm is indicated by the status being different from the command for a period of 15 seconds." Then subcases a and b. So we can create one fault_diagnostic_rule covering both subconditions. Alternatively, split into two separate rules for each condition. The instruction says: "If a requirement has alternatives, split them into separate candidates unless one exact quote supports the combined item." Here the quote "Fan alarm is indicated..." is the overarching condition, and then a and b provide specific alarm levels for each case. We could combine them in one candidate as a diagnostic rule with two conditions. But the evidence quote must be contiguous. Fan alarm is indicated by the status being different from the command for a period of 15 seconds. a. Commanded on, status off:  Level 2 b. Commanded off, status off:  Level 4" That is from the manual. So one candidate can encompass both. knowledge_type: fault_diagnostic_rule. Let's use the full line: "Fan alarm is indicated by the status being different from the command for a period of 15 seconds." then newline and "a. Commanded on, status off:  Level 2" newline "b. I'll quote exactly as in the text, with the double spaces and line breaks indicated by newline characters. But JSON doesn't preserve newlines; we can just use a space. The quote is: "Fan alarm is indicated by the status being different from the command for a period of 15 seconds. a. Commanded on, status off:  Level 2 b. Commanded off, status off:  Level 4" That's fine. page_hint: 171.

That gives us 5 candidates. There is no commissioning_step or parameter_spec explicitly. So we're good.

We should also check the focus scope: 5.19 only, so we cannot include anything from 5.1 context. So OK.

Now we format JSON. Note: The section_id for 5.19.1.1 and 5.19.2.1 etc. We'll use the exact section_id as in the text: "5.19.1.1" for the start/stop, "5.19.2.1" and "5.19.2.2". For the application guidance, it appears under 5.19.1.1 but after the options, so section_id can be "5.19.1.1" as well. We'll set section_title accordingly: "Exhaust Fan Start/Stop" for 5.19.1.1, and "Alarms" for 5.19.2.1 and 5.19.2.2. The section_title for 5.19.2.2 is under "Alarms", but we can just use "Alarms".
